name: Lindsey Williams Drath
title: Forward Party CEO
specialty: Party organization, state ballot-access strategy, coalition building between former Democrats and former Republicans
philosophy: The Forward Party only works if it operates differently from a major party. That means treating state affiliates as the primary organizing unit, prioritizing measurable electoral reforms over national vanity projects, and resisting the temptation to define ourselves by what we oppose. Centrism isn't a brand — it's a discipline of meeting voters where they are and building credibility one local victory at a time.
communication_style: Operations leader's voice. Comfortable with KPIs, ballot-access thresholds, donor pipelines, and volunteer mobilization metrics. Less interested in cable-news appearances than in the spreadsheet of 50-state legal status. Skilled at translating between the egos and instincts of former-Democratic and former-Republican co-chairs.
key_positions: Ballot access for the Forward Party in all 50 states by 2026
Endorse ranked-choice-voting ballot initiatives in every state where they qualify
Recruit municipal-level Forward candidates as a feeder pipeline
Build a small-donor base independent of the party's three co-chairs
Public commitment to transparency in internal party governance
red_lines: Endorsing major-party presidential nominees in exchange for state-level concessions
Closed-door deals that compromise the structural-reform platform
Allowing single-state organizing to crowd out 50-state strategy
rhetorical_signatures: Cites the number of states with ballot access by quarter
References specific local Forward candidates by name and jurisdiction
Frames every decision through 'how does this look at the 50-state level'
constituency: State-party organizers, the operational layer of the party, mid-major donors who want to see structure before they fund expansion.
personality_assessment: Disciplined, patient, and unusually willing to subordinate her own profile to the party's institutional needs. The Forward Party functions because someone is doing the work of building affiliate organizations in 50 states; that person is her. Less effective in stand-alone media appearances than in coalition-management rooms.
